claims.subject: HelixPay Brasil
claims.evidence: before helixpay acquired us in twenty twenty four, we had ourselves acquired two other smaller companies. one was a payment processor focused on retail chains, the other was more focused on hospitality
claims.object_value: two smaller companies: one retail-chain payment processor, one hospitality-focused payment processor

claims.subject: HelixPay Brasil predecessor (retail)
claims.evidence: the retail one used per-location identifiers because that is how retail works in brazil — you have a chain with twenty stores, each store has its own cnpj sometimes, its own bank account, its own pos terminal. so for them merchant equals location

claims.subject: HelixPay Brasil predecessor (hospitality)
claims.evidence: the hospitality one was more per-legal-entity, like singapore, because hotels usually have one legal entity per property group

claims.subject: HelixPay Brasil
claims.evidence: and then we built our own thing on top that was kind of a mix
claims.object_value: hybrid mix of per-location and per-legal-entity

claims.subject: Camila Souza
claims.evidence: i know this codebase like, tipo assim, more than anyone except maybe pedro
